content.claim: Cross-country evidence indicates early tracking has modest-to-negative effects on average achievement while delaying tracking improves social mobility without a clear achievement trade-off.
content.context: Given Hungary's already-highest-in-EU school segregation, any policy alternative to age 10-12 korai szelekció should be judged primarily by whether it reduces SES-based sorting at intake, not only by whether it preserves the apparent ~10% pedagogical value-added advantage for admitted students.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
